nodes.text: Predictable, creator-controlled income — here's exactly what Patreon pays in 2026.

Patreon is the leading membership platform for creators — letting fans support artists, podcasters, writers, and educators with monthly subscriptions. It's the most creator-controlled income stream available: no algorithm, no advertiser approval, just fans paying for your work.
Low barrier, basic supporter recognition. Goal: volume.
Exclusive content, behind-the-scenes, early access. Goal: primary revenue driver.
1-on-1 access, Discord calls, physical goods. Goal: superfan conversion.
Most Patreon revenue comes from the middle tier. Premium tiers convert only 5–10% of total patrons but generate disproportionate revenue. Always offer all three.
Estimate your Patreon income based on your audience size, niche, and conversion rate.
Use our free calculator to estimate your potential income based on your numbers.
Free tool · No sign-up required · Instant results
When do patrons get charged on Patreon?
Patrons are charged on the 1st of each month for monthly memberships, or immediately upon joining for per-creation billing. Creators receive payouts once monthly after a processing period.
What's the minimum viable Patreon audience?

There's no minimum — some creators earn meaningful income with 50–100 highly engaged patrons. What matters more than total audience size is how deeply your existing audience trusts and values your work.
Does Patreon work for all types of creators?

Patreon works best for creators with a loyal, niche audience: podcasters, musicians, writers, educators, and comics artists see the strongest conversion rates. It's less effective for broad entertainment channels with casual viewers.

Patreon offers the highest creator control and lowest platform dependency of any monetization tool. A podcast with 5,000 listeners converting 2% at $8/month earns $800/month in recurring income — independent of any algorithm. Build your Patreon alongside every other platform for income resilience.
